meta.name: Sinhala Design Dependency Model
meta.glyph_naming: Production glyph names follow the lanka-glyphsets namespace convention: "name-sinh" (e.g. ka-sinh), as defined in glyphsets/sinhala-0-kernel.yaml.
sources: docs/sinhala-shape-groups.yaml — WIP shape taxonomy (superseded where this file disagrees)
docs/NotoSansSinhala.glyphspackage — production reference; behavioural classes extracted to docs/sinhala.min.yaml via extract-noto.js

stages.name: System
stages.goal: Decide the type system before drawing letters: base height, stroke and contrast, counter model, overshoot, spacing unit. Deliverable is a set of guides plus rough control drawings of ට and ස — ට for stroke, counter and overshoot; ස for base height (tuned near the Latin x-height in multi-script projects, never 100% matching).
stages.gate: All Stage-0 metrics written down and drawn as guides.

stages.name: Bootstrap
stages.goal: The smallest set that commits the core decisions: the bowl, the body-deciding ස (its body top fixes base height — in multi-script projects tuned near the Latin x-height, similar but never 100% matching, while ට's apex ascends above the body band — and brings the eye-line entry and doubled-body density), the double-bowl ප, the tail (descender depth), the trap, and both vertical mark zones (ි above, ු below) with their first atomic joins (ඩි ඪි). This stage front-loads risk on purpose: every core metric is committed here.
stages.gate: Bowl rhythm reads evenly; base height locked on ස; ඩි ඪි joins convincing; both mark depths frozen.

drills: සට සසඩඪ ටසඩි ඪිටඩසට ඩඪඩි සට ඩසට ඪඩිස ටඪඩඩි සඩඪ ඩටඪිස

stages.name: Bowl derivatives
stages.goal: Everything that follows from Stage 1 with zero new metrics: ච (bowl + eye-line), එ (the trap body standing alone), ය (ප plus the fork), ව (bowl + trap). One new left spacing class opens (L-ja).
stages.gate: All four glyphs sit in Stage-1 rhythm without touching any metric.

stages.name: Arch family and spacing marks
stages.goal: The arch — the script's biggest family — enters via ග, with ා designed alongside it (they share the same right terminal). ත න introduce the ka-eye. The first spacing marks (් kodiya, ෙ, ං) unlock real words, and the ් system branches: the rahena wrap (ට්) and the trap-top alternate (ඪ්) are decided against their Stage-1 hosts. The first ි family taken from a designed host: සි (the ඝි family, introduced by its first available member).
stages.gate: First real Sinhala words read naturally; arch terminal and ා are indistinguishable in rhythm.

stages.name: High-frequency completion
stages.goal: The stage that makes the font write Sinhala: ක ද උ අ ර ල, the hooks ැ ෑ, the vowel composites ආ ඇ ඈ and ේ ො ෝ ෛ, the irregular ර forms (රි ර් රැ), the first wave of atomic ි families (කි ගි දි ලි පි), and the special ු form (කු ගු තු) plus දු. Text coverage jumps to the majority of running Sinhala.
stages.gate: Full sentences set cleanly; frequency coverage measured against a corpus, not assumed.

sentences: අද උදේ අපේ දරුවා පාසලට ගියා.

stages.name: Loops, diagonals and long marks
stages.goal: The loop enters via ඔ (then ම), ණ combines arch + diagonal, ජ ඡ put the diagonal on the ප skeleton, ඉ ථ ඨ complete the medium shapes. Long marks ී ූ unlock the ී forms of every family designed so far, and the ja-shape ් alternate arrives on ජ්.
stages.gate: Loop counters match the counter model; ී ූ read as the long counterparts of frozen ි ු forms.

stages.name: Aspirates and dense derivatives
stages.goal: Single-delta children of frozen skeletons: ධ බ ඛ on the bowl, ඕ ඹ on the loop, භ හ ශ ඝ ෂ on the arch/eye system, ළ ෆ ඵ ඒ ඊ ඌ completing their families. Spacing is pure inheritance; the density ceiling (ඝ ෂ ඹ) gets tested against the colour of Stage 1.
stages.gate: No spacing class needed retuning; dense glyphs hold the page colour.

stages.name: The hang system
stages.goal: The hang element enters on ඳ and propagates: ඬ ඟ ඤ ඥ ඦ ඣ. The da_forms note applies — the bottoms of ද ඳ ඤ ඥ (and later න්‍ද) must stay consistent. Their ි families (ඳි, ඤ/ඥි) close the nasal set.
stages.gate: Hang depth identical across the set; da-form bottoms interchangeable.

stages.name: Completion sweep
stages.goal: Close the base × mark matrix before conjuncts: the body-mark tail (ෘ ෲ ෟ ෳ and the composite ෞ), the systematic ් sweep across every base (kodiya / rahena / trap / ja form per assignment), and completion of every ි/ී family member whose host arrived after the family was frozen. Full-grid QA in the glyph chart tool is the gate.
stages.gate: Every cell of the sinhala-glyph-chart base grid renders correctly or is a documented exception.

stages.name: Conjunct layer
stages.goal: The ZWJ layer: rakaransaya ්‍ර (with its exception list), yansaya ්‍ය, repaya ර්‍, the ක්‍ෂ ligature, the classic conjuncts (න්‍ද ත්‍ථ න්‍ථ ට්‍ඨ ද්‍ර), touching clusters, and the conjunct ි/ී forms (ක්‍රී ශ්‍රී ද්‍රි න්‍දි — the ග්‍රි group of the shape taxonomy). Per-glyph atomic-vs-GPOS decisions are recorded here.
stages.gate: Shaping-engine tests pass (sinh/sin2); every conjunct either drawn, composed, or a documented exclusion.

sentences: ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ ක්‍රිකට් ක්‍රීඩාව ඉතා ප්‍රසිද්ධයි.

stages.name: Long tail and polish
stages.goal: The archaic and rare set (ඍ ඎ ඏ ඐ ඞ ඓ ඖ ඃ), kunddaliya ෴, punctuation and numeral policy, then the kerning pass across spacing-class representatives. Weight/width derivatives replay this same graph from Stage 0.
stages.gate: Level target inventories (glyphsets/*.yaml) fully covered for the shipping level.

ai.construction: The control glyph. A single open bowl on the baseline: one continuous stroke, aperture per the counter model. Everything in the font is calibrated against this drawing. Note: ට's apex ascends above the general body area — it is deliberately taller than the base-height band, which ස (next) decides.
checks: Counter reads open at text size; overshoot symmetric top/bottom.
Sidebearings define the spacing unit for L-tta and R-bowl.

ai.construction: The body-deciding glyph, drawn immediately after ට: eye-line entry into a doubled body — the widest simple glyph and the first density problem. Its body top fixes base height (in multi-script projects tuned near the Latin x-height — similar, never 100% matching), while ට's apex ascends above it. Introduces the eye_line element, the L-e entry class and the R-pa terminal that ප ය ඝ ෂ inherit.
checks: Base height locked on ස's body top; in a Latin companion, cross-check against the x-height (close, not equal).
Even colour beside ට at text size; L-e and R-pa sidebearings set.

ai.construction: Two joined bowls sitting on the base height ස decided — the flat double-bowl top lands exactly on the body band. Introduces the double_bowl skeleton and the L-pa entry that අ උ ද ව හ inherit; the right terminal inherits R-pa from ස.
checks: Top of ප sits exactly on base height; two counters read even.
L-pa sidebearing set; width believable next to ස.

ai.construction: ට reworked with ස's eye-line left entry, plus the knotted tail below the baseline. Introduces the tail element and commits tail_depth.
checks: Tail knot (grantika) doesn't clog at small sizes.
Left entry matches the L-e rhythm set by ස.

ai.construction: ඩ plus the trap closure on the right; its right side must land in the ඨ ථ එ ඵ terminal family (R-trap), which it introduces.
checks: Trap counter vs bowl counter balance; R-trap sidebearing set.

ai.construction: The above-join convention, first typographic form only — the form that rides the bowl tops (ට ච ඩ class). Which of the 3+ forms this is must be stated in the project spec. Commits mark_height.
checks: Clearance over base height; loop legible at small sizes.

ai.construction: Atomic ි over the open-bowl hosts; introduced on ඩි. ච joins in Stage 2, the ී long forms (ටී චී ඩී) in Stage 5.
checks: ඩි join reads as one drawn glyph, not a collision.

ai.construction: Atomic ි over the trap-topped hosts; introduced on ඪි. Remaining hosts (ඨ ථ ඵ) complete in Stages 5–6; conjunct hosts (ට්‍ඨ ත්‍ථ න්‍ථ) in Stage 9.
checks: The ි loop negotiates the open trap without closing it.

ai.construction: The regular below-join, tested on සු. Commits mark_depth — a shallower zone than tail_depth; the two must be decided together.
checks: සු and ටු sit at identical depth; no collision with ඩ-type tails in setting.

ai.construction: ට's bowl with ඩ's eye-line entry; no tail. Pure recombination.
checks: Indistinguishable rhythm from ට in a drill line.

ai.construction: The trap body standing alone — the archetype refined from its first appearance on ඪ. Skeleton for ඒ ඨ ඵ later.
checks: Reads as a stable independent letter, not a fragment of ඪ.

ai.construction: ප plus the internal fork; introduces the L-ja entry class (ජ ර ල ළ ෆ … inherit).
checks: Fork doesn't darken the middle; L-ja sidebearing set.

ai.construction: Bowl with the trap element on the right; inherits everything.
checks: Distinct from ච ට at a glance in the drill string.

ai.construction: The top-terminal wrap ි — for bases whose top terminal forces the loop to wrap around it (the same top-terminal logic as the rahena ්). Noto Sans Sinhala fuses every one of these as a single ligature glyph (yI-sinh, mI-sinh …). Introduced on යි; later hosts inherit as they arrive.
checks: Wrap negotiates each terminal without closing its counter.

ai.construction: The default KODIYA flag form (as on ක්). Three sibling decisions follow: the rahena wrap (hal-rahena), the trap-top alternate (hal-trap) and the ja-shape alternate (hal-ja).
checks: Flag clears mark_height; legible after narrow and wide bases (ක් vs ස්).

ai.construction: The naked arch — introduces the skeleton and BOTH spacing classes of the script's largest family. The right terminal here is the exact model for ා.
checks: R-arch terminal frozen; this stroke ending is now law for 13 glyphs and 3 marks.

ai.construction: Designed WITH ග: the same right terminal, standing as a spacing mark.
checks: In ගා the two terminals are twins; spacing identical to R-arch.

ai.construction: ග plus the closed ka-eye; introduces eye_ka and the L-ka entry.
checks: Eye aperture per counter model; eye joint (akshi dhāraka sandi) clean.

ai.construction: ත's construction with the mid-form variation; pure sibling.
checks: ත/න contrast unambiguous at text size.

ai.construction: The pre-base spacing mark; its left profile belongs to the L-ga class (source groups ෙ with ග ඟ ඤ ඥ …), so it follows ග.
checks: ෙ + base spacing consistent across L classes; never collides with L-e entries.

ai.construction: A filled/open dot at base height — trivial, very frequent, and needed for real words (ගං, සං-).
checks: Optically round; weight matches stroke, not geometry.

ai.construction: The ඝි-family convention introduced by its first DESIGNED member — සි (the family key ඝ only arrives in Stage 6 and inherits). Demonstrates the representative-introduces-family principle.
checks: සි reads as one glyph; convention transferable to ඝ later.

ai.construction: The RAHENA ් form: on bases with a TOP TERMINAL the kodiya flag cannot land, so ් wraps the terminal and the pair fuses into one glyph (Noto: tt-sinh, m-sinh …). Introduced on ට්. ර් belongs to ra-forms, not here. ඔ ඕ share the top terminal visually but, being independent vowels, never take ්.
checks: One wrap convention across all hosts; kodiya/rahena assignment table complete.

ai.construction: The ් alternate over trap-topped bases (Noto virama.alt1 over its pha-shape class) — the flag must clear the open trap. Introduced on ඪ්; ඨ ථ ඵ inherit in Stages 5–6.
checks: Flag clears the trap without closing it; distinct from kodiya at text size.

ai.construction: The eye variant of ත — the same skeleton with the doubled-eye construction.
checks: ක ත න form a rhythm family in a drill line.

ai.construction: Introduces the u_bottom skeleton and the R-u terminal (උ ඤ ඥ ඳ ැ ෑ inherit). The da-form bottom must stay consistent across ද ඳ ඤ ඥ න්‍ද (da_forms note in the shape taxonomy).
checks: R-u terminal frozen; bottom curve reusable verbatim in ඳ.

ai.construction: The u_bottom standing alone with its opening stroke.
checks: Sibling contrast with ද clear at small sizes.

ai.construction: The complex member of the උ group; introduces the R-a terminal (ල ළ inherit). The most frequent letter in the language — worth a disproportionate share of stage time.
checks: Reads instantly as අ; R-a sidebearing set.

ai.construction: Introduces the diagonal skeleton/element. ර is deliberately early: it is top-five frequency, and the whole Stage 9 conjunct layer (rakaransaya, repaya) plus five irregular combined forms hang off it.
checks: Diagonal angle recorded as a system constant; R-diag set.

ai.construction: L-ja entry (from ය) into the R-a terminal (from අ); everything inherited.
checks: ල අ terminals interchangeable.

ai.construction: The below-baseline hook; commits hook_depth. Its right profile belongs to the R-u class (source groups ැ ෑ with උ ද ඳ), hence parent ද.
checks: Depth distinct from tail_depth and mark_depth; no collision in ගැ දැ.

ai.construction: ැ plus the length stroke; pure derivative.
checks: ැ/ෑ contrast survives small sizes.

ai.construction: The irregular ර system: රි ර් රැ are unique drawn forms, not mark attachments (the shape taxonomy gives ර-forms their own group). රී රූ complete in Stage 5 when ී ූ exist. Noto goes as far as REUSING the ැ glyph for රු (ra + u-sign is substituted by the aela-pilla-style hook) — evidence of how fused this system is.
checks: Each form clearly a member of the ර family; none reads as base+mark.

ai.construction: අ + the ා terminal, drawn as one letter. Near-zero cost.
checks: Join point invisible; spacing = අ left, ා right.

ai.construction: අ + the ැ hook integrated at the terminal.
checks: Hook depth identical to standalone ැ.

ai.construction: ඇ + length stroke, exactly as ෑ derives from ැ.
checks: Consistent with ෑ.

ai.construction: Canonical assembly ෙ + ් (U+0DDA decomposes to 0DD9 0DCA). Composite or reference, per project policy.
checks: Flag position identical to base+් cases.

ai.construction: Assembly ෙ + base + ා (U+0DDC = 0DD9 0DCF).
checks: Both halves keep their class spacing around any base.

ai.construction: ො + ් (U+0DDD = 0DDC 0DCA).
checks: Consistent with ේ flag.

ai.construction: Two kombuwa; rhythm between the pair is the only new decision.
checks: Pair gap tighter than letter-space, looser than stroke gap.

ai.construction: The arch-eye ි convention, archetype කි. The ී forms complete in Stage 5.
checks: One convention fits all three eyes without per-glyph fudging.

ai.construction: The naked-arch ි convention introduced on ගි; ඟ ශ ෆ inherit in Stages 6–7.
checks: Distinct from fam-i-ki at a glance (eye vs no eye).

ai.construction: The ද ි convention; conjunct hosts (ද්‍රි) join in Stage 9.
checks: දි loop respects the u_bottom counter.

ai.construction: Introduced on ලි; ළ inherits in Stage 6.
checks: Convention holds over both the ල and ළ terminals.

ai.construction: Introduced on පි. NOTE: the source table for this family is marked unclear (rows repeat, no clean i/ii split) — verify membership.
checks: පි join clean over the double bowl.

ai.construction: The SECOND typographic form of ු — the right-joined form on arch-family terminals. Introduced on කු; භ ශ ඟ inherit in Stages 6–7. ළු is NOT a member: it is an irregular fused form of its own (entry llu-form).
checks: කු reads as one glyph; join point identical across hosts.

ai.construction: Irregular: ු fuses into ද's bowl. The shape taxonomy files දු beside ක්‍රී ශ්‍රී (the ග්‍රි group) — an oddity flagged in `gaps`; treated here as its own one-host family.
checks: දු/du contrast with ඳ and ඳු unambiguous.

ai.construction: Introduces the loop (pāsha) on the bowl; opens BOTH round spacing classes.
checks: Loop counter per counter model; L-o/R-round sidebearings set.

ai.construction: The loop in body position — ඔ's decision applied mid-letter.
checks: ම ඔ loop sizes consistent.

ai.construction: Arch construction (න) carrying the diagonal (ර) — the first cross-family recombination.
checks: Diagonal angle matches the ර constant.

ai.construction: High-density curled form on the ප skeleton; right terminal joins R-arch.
checks: Density holds colour beside low-density neighbours (ට ර).

ai.construction: Trap body with the L-ja entry; completes ඨි-family host ථි immediately.
checks: R-trap inheritance exact.

ai.construction: ඪ without the tail, on the trap body; ඨි completes.
checks: Sibling logic ඨ/ඪ mirrors ට/ඩ.

ai.construction: The diagonal applied to the ප skeleton; right side joins ර's R-diag class.
checks: Diagonal constant respected.

ai.construction: ජ's construction with the closed/trap right; single delta.
checks: Reads apart from ජ at text size.

ai.construction: The long counterpart of ි. Unlocks the ී member of EVERY family frozen so far (කී තී නී ගී සී ටී ඩී රී …) — a sweep, not new decisions.
checks: ි/ී contrast systematic across all families.

ai.construction: Long ු; same depth, added length stroke. Unlocks කූ ගූ රූ දූ sweeps.
checks: Depth identical to ු.

ai.construction: The diagonal-host ි convention; ඦ inherits in Stage 7.
checks: Loop clears the diagonal apex.

ai.construction: The ් alternate over the ja-shape diagonal tops (Noto virama.alt2 over its ja-shape class). Introduced on ජ්; ඦ inherits in Stage 7.
checks: Flag negotiates the diagonal apex; consistent across ඡ ජ ඦ.

ai.construction: ට plus the inner stroke; pure single delta.
checks: Inner stroke doesn't choke the counter.

ai.construction: The බ-group archetype (yaml බ group: ඛ බ); L-tta entry with the round right.
checks: R-round inheritance from ඔ exact.

ai.construction: බ plus the aspirate mark-stroke; single delta.
checks: Distinct from බ at text sizes.

ai.construction: ඔ + the ා-style terminal, as ආ derives from අ.
checks: Terminal twin-test against ා.

ai.construction: ම plus the connecting stroke — the prenasalized member of the loop family.
checks: Density: the darkest loop glyph must still match page colour.

ai.construction: Arch + ka-eye (from ත) entered with the eye-line (from ස) — a documented double-element recombination, both already frozen.
checks: Both inherited elements verbatim; nothing new introduced.

ai.construction: The eye_ka system with the looped under-entry; frequent — schedule generous review time.
checks: හ/න/ත family rhythm test.

ai.construction: Arch with the L-ga entry curl and the inner hook; single delta on ග.
checks: ශ ෂ ස triplet distinctness.

ai.construction: The ස construction closed and densified; inherits the frozen fam-i-ghi convention (ඝි) from සි.
checks: High-density benchmark: darkest allowed colour.

ai.construction: ස plus the crossing bar; joins fam-i-pi for ෂි.
checks: Bar weight = thin-stroke constant.

ai.construction: ල plus the retroflex knot; ළි completes fam-i-li. The irregular ළු follows as its own entry (llu-form).
checks: Knot legible.

ai.construction: ප reworked with the R-arch terminal; loan-sound letter.
checks: Terminal from R-arch verbatim.

ai.construction: Trap body with the L-pa entry; ඵි completes fam-i-tthi hosts.
checks: R-trap inheritance.

ai.construction: එ + the halanta-style stroke, as ේ derives from ෙ.
checks: Consistent with ේ logic.

ai.construction: ඉ carrying the diagonal — the taxonomy files ඊ in BOTH the ප and ර groups; primary here is the ර system's diagonal.
checks: Diagonal constant; distinct from රී.

ai.construction: උ plus the length tail — the ූ logic at letter scale.
checks: Tail depth consistent with ෟ-style descenders.

ai.construction: The eye-topped-arch ි convention for භ හ.
checks: One convention fits both without collision with the eye.

ai.construction: Irregular fused form of its own, like දු — NOT ළ plus an attached ු. Visually it belongs to the trap family: an එ/ඵ-like body carrying the ඪ-style top trap. Draw it from the trap-body archetype (එ) with the frozen trap element; ළ contributes identity, not outline. ළූ adds the length stroke.
checks: Spaces with the trap family (R-trap logic), not with ළ.
ළු/ළූ contrast survives small sizes; no confusion with ඵ.

ai.construction: ද plus the hang — introduces the element for the whole nasal set. da-form bottom stays verbatim.
checks: Hang depth recorded as the system constant.

ai.construction: ඩ plus the hang; tail and hang must coexist below the baseline.
checks: tail_depth vs hang depth collision check.

ai.construction: ග plus the hang; ඟි and ඟු complete their families.
checks: Hang constant; arch untouched.

ai.construction: L-ga entry + hang + the da-form bottom (da_forms list: ද ඳ ඤ ඥ න්‍ද share it).
checks: Bottom interchangeable with ද ඳ.

ai.construction: ඤ's construction with the doubled body; single delta.
checks: Density vs ඝ benchmark.

ai.construction: ජ plus the hang; completes fam-i-ji hosts.
checks: Hang + diagonal don't collide.

ai.construction: Two bowl bodies (ච-type) fused side by side — introduces the twin_body element: the Gaṇḍa joint between two halves that the ligated conjuncts (න්‍ද ත්‍ථ ක්‍ව ග්‍ධ …) inherit in Stage 9. A rare letter, but drawing it solves the fusion construction once, two stages before the conjunct layer needs it eleven more times.
checks: The joint (gaṇḍa) reads as one construction, not a collision.
Joint recorded as the system constant for Stage 9 conjuncts.

ai.construction: The ඳ ි convention; conjunct hosts join in Stage 9. Source rows mix ො forms (ඳො ඳ්‍රො) — verify.
checks: Distinct from දි at a glance.

ai.construction: The nasal-set ි convention; න්‍දි න්‍ද්‍රි join in Stage 9 (source files them in this family).
checks: Convention scales to the wide ඥ body.

ai.construction: Below-right body mark at mark_depth; right profile joins R-arch per the taxonomy.
checks: කෘ තෘ පෘ set cleanly.

ai.construction: ෘ + length stroke.
checks: Consistent with ු/ූ logic.

ai.construction: The gayanukitta descender-tail mark.
checks: Depth family with ඌ tail.

ai.construction: ෟ + length stroke.
Assembly ෙ + base + ෟ (U+0DDE = 0DD9 0DDF).

Mechanical pass: every form-family member whose host arrived after the family froze (ටී ඩී චී, ථි ඨි ඵි+ී, ඝි, ළි ළී, ෂි, ඟි ශි ෆි+ී, ඦි, භු ශු ඟු, කූ ගූ තූ …) is produced by applying the frozen convention. No new decisions permitted; anything that resists the convention goes to `gaps`.
checks: Full base × ි ී ු ූ grid in sinhala-glyph-chart renders correctly.

ai.construction: Apply the correct ් form to every base per the four-form assignment (kodiya default / rahena wrap / trap alternate / ja alternate; the ක් group of the shape taxonomy lists the kodiya set). Word-final forms checked in running text.
checks: Every base has exactly one assigned ් form; no unassigned base remains.

ai.construction: The below-base ර form. Encoding: base + ් + ZWJ + ර. Apply the exception list from sinhala-definitions.js (rakaransayaExceptions). Atomic vs GPOS per glyph_construction policy.
checks: Depth = conjunct_depth; exceptions excluded in features.

ai.construction: The post-base ය form; base + ් + ZWJ + ය.
checks: Right-join consistent across L-classes of the following context.

ai.construction: The above-base ර form (reph). Encoding: ර + ් + ZWJ + base. Apply repayaExceptions from sinhala-definitions.js.
checks: Sits inside mark_height; collision test with ි ී ේ.

ai.construction: The ක්‍ෂ ligature; its ි ී forms belong to fam-i-pi per the source.
checks: Reads as one letter; width believable.

classic-conjuncts.display: න්‍ද ත්‍ථ න්‍ථ ට්‍ඨ ද්‍ර න්‍ද්‍ර

ai.construction: The classic stacked/fused conjuncts. The twin-body fusion convention is INHERITED from ඣ (element twin_body) — reuse its joint, do not re-derive it. න්‍ද joins the da_forms consistency set. Their ි ී forms extend fam-i-tthi, fam-i-nyi and fam-i-di per the source tables.
checks: da-form bottom verbatim in න්‍ද; ත්‍ථි matches fam-i-tthi.

ai.construction: Touching consonant clusters. NOTE the encoding difference: touching = c1 + ZWJ + ් + c2; ligated conjuncts = c1 + ් + ZWJ + c2.
checks: Shaping tests distinguish the two encodings correctly.

conjunct-i-forms.display: ක්‍රි ක්‍රී ශ්‍රී ද්‍රි න්‍දි (ග්‍රි group)

ai.construction: ි ී over conjunct hosts — the ග්‍රි group of the shape taxonomy (ක්‍රී ච්‍රී ප්‍රී ශ්‍රී). Extends the frozen family conventions to two-storey hosts.
checks: Mark clears the conjunct stack; no re-decisions of frozen forms.

ai.construction: Vocalic-r letter on the ප-group skeleton per the taxonomy.
ඍ + length element.
Vocalic-l letter; L-ga entry per the taxonomy.
ඏ + length element.
Archaic velar nasal on the ට-group skeleton.
The ai letter; derivation from the එ system — verify with designer.
The au letter; ඔ system + tail logic.
Two ං dots stacked at the right.
Traditional section mark; spiral construction (cf. Śṅkaya).
Kern between spacing-CLASS representatives first (7 left × 8 right pairs), then push exceptions down to members only where the class solution fails. Punctuation and numeral policy decided here.
checks: Class kerning covers ≥90% of pairs before any glyph exception is added.
